frameworks.name: combined brain stimulation and cognitive training
frameworks.one_line: tDCS paired with multitasking practice to induce persistent, transferable gains

frameworks.name: frontoparietal-subcortical multitasking network
frameworks.one_line: prefrontal, parietal, and subcortical activity supports multitasking improvement

core_question: How does the brain support transfer of multitasking training gains to untrained tasks following prefrontal tDCS?
key_assumptions: prefrontal cortex is a critical site for multitasking control
transfer reflects shared neural substrates between trained and untrained tasks
manipulations: tDCS site (left vs right prefrontal cortex, 1 mA)
pre- vs post-training fMRI and 1-month follow-up
key_findings: transfer to a visual search task emerged after left or right prefrontal tDCS plus multitasking training
right-hemisphere stimulation linked visual search improvement to activity changes in right DLPFC, intraparietal sulcus, and cerebellum
interpretations: prefrontal tDCS combined with multitasking training produces durable, transferable cognitive gains
individual differences in transfer are tied to a right-lateralized frontoparietal-cerebellar network
